title: Local download reliability: failed-download resume, single-stream throughput ceiling, and speed/ETA feedback
description:
User-reported as "downloads are fucked again". Investigation 2026-07-20 found THREE separate defects plus two dead ends. Findings are marked PROVEN vs SUSPECTED - do not treat a hypothesis as fact.

SCOPE NOTE: user explicitly asked for ONE card, not several. Everything below stays here. Do not split.

=== F1. PROVEN - a FAILED download deletes its resumable partial ===
routs/downloadManager.js:662 in download(): `await fs.remove(this.localPath)` is UNCONDITIONAL.
Live loss observed: Chroma Flash (chroma1-hd-flash, 17GB dep) reached 5.66GB on disk, socket stalled ~16:08:26, watchdog force-failed at 16:09:26, and the 5.66GB .safetensors was scrubbed. Only the 297-byte .cubricdl sidecar survived.

The scrub is NOT an oversight - it is load-bearing. Comment cites MPI-258 Bug 2: NDH resumes with `Range: bytes=<n>-` on a file opened in append mode; when R2/Cloudflare answers 200 (full body) instead of 206 (partial), it appends the WHOLE body onto the partial -> SHA256 mismatch. Observed live on the 25GB LTX transformer. Resume was removed on purpose.

USER'S FRAMING (correct, adopt it): cancel is INTENT -> delete the bytes. Failure is ACCIDENT -> keep them. App shutdown mid-download should also resume next attempt.

The real fix is NOT "stop deleting on failure". It is: resume only when the server PROVES it is resuming. Check the response status - 206 = append is safe, 200 = server ignored Range, must restart clean. That status check is exactly what MPI-258 lacked, which is why unconditional delete was the only safe option at the time.
Safety net already exists: MPI-296 hashes SHA256 WHILE streaming and _verifySha256 (downloadManager.js:599) compares to expected, so a bad resume is caught before a corrupt weight installs.
OPEN UNKNOWN: can the downloader library (NDH) expose the HTTP response status? That decides whether F1 is a small fix or a library swap. NOT YET INVESTIGATED.

=== F2. PROVEN - single-stream throughput is ~half the user's link ===
routs/downloadManager.js:2 header: "Non-blocking, single-stream download manager." aria2 is RunPod-ONLY (only refs are routes/remotePodLifecycle.js:148/181/542) - local downloads are plain Node HTTP streams, one socket per file.
Measured live: 574MB in 30s = ~19 MB/s. User's link does a sustained ~40 MB/s in a browser (oscillates, but 40 is typical). ~48% of available bandwidth.
The premise at downloadManager.js:412 - "a single R2 stream already saturates a typical link" - is FALSE for this connection. Single-TCP throughput is bounded by RTT and window size, not link capacity.
downloadManager.js:408 sets 3 PARALLEL DEPS, which does nothing here: Chroma Flash resolved to ONE dep (log: `_startPendingDeps: 1 queued deps`). Parallel-deps != parallel-connections. A single large weight gets exactly one stream.
Fix direction: multi-connection range-split for a single large dep (what aria2 gives the Pod, what browsers do). Compounds with F1 - a 17GB file at half speed spends twice as long exposed to the stall that deletes it.

=== F3. PROVEN (by absence) - no speed or ETA anywhere in the UI ===
User could not tell a slow download from a hung one; throughput had to be measured by an agent running stat(1) against the file on disk. This is why the original complaint was ambiguous for six sessions.
Data already exists: _speedSamples is live in js/services/downloadService.js (MPI-94), server broadcasts download:progress. The tile just renders pct and nothing else.
USER DECISION: put speed + ETA in the SLIDE-OVER (detail panel), NOT the tile. Tiles are small and fixed-height (see _tileState comment in MpiModelManager.js) and would need a redesign; the slide-over already has room and already renders Cancel. Do not redesign the cards.
This is observability, not decoration - it is what makes F1/F2 and the stall self-diagnosing instead of agent-diagnosed.

=== F4. SUSPECTED, INSTRUMENTED, NOT YET CONFIRMED - missed/stale completion notification ===
Two opposite failures on consecutive installs:
- Install A (user unfocused): got OS notification, THEN a stale in-app toast on refocus. Toast named "Krea 2" - a model installed EARLIER, not the one just finished.
- Install B (Krea 2 Turbo, user focused): NO toast and NO OS notification at all.
Two candidate mechanisms, NOT separated:
(a) downloadService.js:469 `const isUW = !data.modelId || ...` - per-dep completes carry modelId:null so the whole `if (!isUW)` body is skipped INCLUDING `Events.emit('download:complete')`, which is what notificationService listens on. Delivery depends entirely on the model-level event arriving as a distinct event.
(b) the cascade-toast loop at downloadService.js:494-508 walks every model that flipped installed after re-sync and toasts it; it skips data.modelId but has no guard against re-toasting on a LATER re-sync, and re-sync runs on every model-level complete.
A TEMP TRACE LINE IS ALREADY IN THE TREE - see "instrumentation" below.

=== DEAD ENDS - do not re-investigate ===
- "The MPI-310 plugin agent broke downloads" (user's initial theory): DISPROVEN. The plugin term at MpiModelManager.js:1117 APPENDS to the repaint signature (`+ '##plugins:'`) - it cannot suppress a model term. Per-model jobSig already includes job.status and job.phase. Every mechanism found predates MPI-310.
- downloadQueueActive predicate mismatch: REAL but NOT the cause of the Install-chip screenshot. Three different definitions of "active" exist (downloadService.js:466 `downloading` only; :395 downloading|installing|pending|queued; MpiModelManager.js:318 downloading|installing) and :466 flips false when a model enters the pip/installing phase. Cosmetically harmless so far - two clean installs showed correct CANCEL + live bar throughout. Worth tidying to one predicate, LOW priority, not the bug.
- pip thrash during custom-node install: REAL but explains DURATION only, not the Install chip. kjnodes and Impact-Pack fight over matplotlib/transformers - each `--upgrade -r requirements.txt` pass installs newest, then the pin pass uninstalls and reinstalls the pinned version. ~2.5min of churn with zero UI feedback (log 23:19:05-23:21:19). Real perf bug, separate concern, do not conflate with the chip.

=== STILL UNEXPLAINED ===
The ORIGINAL symptom - a card showing the `Install` chip WHILE a download is live at ~90% - has NOT reproduced. Two clean installs (Krea 2 Turbo, Chroma Flash) both held CANCEL + a live bar correctly.
Best remaining theory: _tileState falls through to the final `return ...chip--available` when st.job is absent, and the idle partial-progress path (_computePartial, clamped to 0.99, excludes custom_nodes) can paint a high-90s bar with no job behind it. That would look exactly like the screenshot: frozen bar, no Cancel, Install chip.
To reproduce you need a LONG installing window, which needs custom nodes NOT yet on disk. Currently impossible: all 14 declared node deps are already extracted in engine/.../custom_nodes/. Would require deliberately removing one (comfyui-krea2edit is small, only Krea2 edit uses it) - user has NOT authorised this.

=== INSTRUMENTATION IN THE TREE - MUST BE REMOVED ===
js/services/downloadService.js:460 - a `[trace]` clientLogger.info line at the TOP of the download:complete handler, deliberately BEFORE the isUW gate so per-dep (modelId:null) events are visible. Logs modelId, depId, the full job list with statuses, and document.hasFocus().
UNCOMMITTED and TEMPORARY. Do not let it ship into 1.2.0. Either revert it or land it as an explicit temp-trace commit.
It has produced NO DATA YET - it was added while the app was already running, and the renderer was never reloaded, so the live app is on old code. Needs a reload + one more install.
When reading it back: the log is SHARED with another agent's concurrent session, so grep the unique `[trace]` string, not `[downloadService]`.

=== KNOWN-BAD, UNRELATED, FOUND IN PASSING ===
js/data/modelConstants/nodesDeps.js:122-124 - a dep named 'ComfyUI Impact Subpack' has `filename: 'comfyui-frame-interpolation'`. Looks like copy-paste. Both folders exist on disk so nothing is broken today. NOT fixed, NOT carded (user asked for no new cards) - flagged here so it is not lost.

=== SUGGESTED ORDER ===
1. F1 (resume on failure) - highest value, user cares most, smallest surface IF NDH exposes response status.
2. F3 (speed/ETA in slide-over) - small, and makes everything else observable.
3. F2 (multi-connection) - biggest win on wall-clock, biggest change.
4. F4 - needs the trace line to produce data first.

events.note: Raised by user as 'downloads are fucked again' after an install appeared stuck at 90%. Investigation found F1 (failed download deletes 5.66GB resumable partial - observed live on Chroma Flash), F2 (single-stream ~19MB/s vs user's ~40MB/s link), F3 (no speed/ETA in UI at all), F4 (missed/stale completion notification, instrumented but unconfirmed). User's initial theory that the MPI-310 plugin agent caused it was disproven. Original 90% Install-chip symptom did not reproduce and remains unexplained. A temp [trace] line is uncommitted in downloadService.js:460 and has produced no data yet - app was never reloaded.

F1 SHIPPED (unit-verified, live-unverified): failed/stalled/shutdown downloads now KEEP the partial and the next attempt resumes via an explicit Range request; user cancel still deletes. Key discoveries: (1) the installed NDH clears __isResumed on a 200-not-206 answer and TRUNCATES instead of appending - the MPI-258 Bug 2 corruption cannot recur on this version, so the unconditional scrub's premise is gone; (2) the 5.66GB Chroma Flash loss was NDH's own removeOnStop/removeOnFail defaults via the watchdog's stop(), not our scrub; (3) tests/download-completion.test.cjs testDownloaderResumesMarkedPartial was an ORPHANED SPEC of the original ResumableDownloader contract - failing before this change, passing after; implementation was shaped to match it (explicit resumeFromFile with marker-verified state + url guard) rather than rewriting the test. Resumed streams skip the MPI-296 incremental hash (tail-only = garbage) and fall back to full disk re-read in _verifySha256. 416 scrubs the oversized-garbage partial. cancelAllDownloads (SIGTERM/SIGINT) uses new stopKeep() so app shutdown preserves partials. All 8 adjacent test files green, 0 failures. LIVE VERIFICATION PENDING: needs app restart (routes/ = server process, no hot reload) + a real stall/quit mid-download + retry showing Range resume. F2 (multi-connection) and F3 (speed/ETA slide-over) not started.

F3 SHIPPED (needs renderer reload to see): live speed + ETA row in the model detail slide-over while a download runs - e.g. '24.1 MB/s | 5.6 / 17.0 GB | ~8 min left'. Template row #detail-dlstats above the actions footer (NOT inside .mpi-detail__actions - that is a flex row with flex:1 children, a div inside would sit beside Cancel at half width). Panel is not rebuilt on progress ticks (rebuildDetail:false), so the download:progress listener patches the row's textContent in place; ETA parses the backend's formatted speed string back to bytes/s rather than re-deriving a client rate. Hidden during verifying/installing phases (stale rate would lie). User chose slide-over over tile placement - tiles are small/fixed-height and would need a redesign.

F2 RETRACTED - do not build multi-connection downloads on this evidence. The F3 speed readout, on its first live download, showed SDXL Realistic pulling 40.7 MB/s on the SAME single-stream code path that gave Chroma Flash ~19 MB/s. So the ~19 MB/s was object/route-specific on R2 (the same object that also stalled twice), NOT a single-TCP-window ceiling, and the downloadManager.js:412 premise ('a single R2 stream already saturates a typical link') is CORRECT for this link after all. If throughput is revisited, the question is why that one R2 object was slow+stall-prone, not how to parallelise connections.

F4 DIAGNOSED (fix not yet written). Trace output, 13s apart, both focus=false:
  17:01:59 modelId=null depId=chroma1-hd-flash jobs=chroma-flash:downloading
  17:02:12 modelId=chroma-flash depId=undefined jobs=none
By the time the MODEL-level complete arrived, state.downloadJobs was ALREADY EMPTY - the dep-level complete (modelId:null) cleared the job 13s early, so downloadService.js:461 `state.downloadJobs.find(...)` matched nothing and the notification/toast path ran with no job to anchor on. Explains BOTH symptoms: no toast at all (nothing to update) and a stale toast naming a previously-installed model (the cascade loop at :494-508 walks MODELS with no job anchor). NOT a server broadcast race - the server emitted both events correctly. Next step: find the site that clears the job on a dep-level complete and guard it on data.modelId, the same guard the failed path already has at :570.

Chroma Flash DID complete successfully after the retry: 17,800,038,288 bytes on disk, marker cleared, SHA256 verify passed.

F1 LIVE-VERIFIED by the user (quit-mid-download path). ILL Anime Beauty: quit the app at 20% -> BOTH the 1.41GB partial AND its .cubricdl marker survived on disk (under the old code NDH's removeOnStop/removeOnFail defaults would have eaten them) -> restart -> Install -> resumed at 25% and kept climbing; file measured 1.41GB at quit then 3.18GB mid-resume, never reset to 0. Range resume against R2 works end to end.

Still unverified sub-paths: (a) CANCEL must still DELETE the partial (20-second check, not yet run - a regression here would silently fill the drive with abandoned partials); (b) the stall/watchdog path (cannot be staged on demand - only ever fired on the one Chroma object).

Also: the '[trace]' instrumentation in js/services/downloadService.js has been REVERTED (it produced its evidence, F4 is diagnosed). And routes/downloadManager.js now logs which branch download() took ('resuming <dep> from N.NNGB on disk' / 'discarding unusable partial ... clean start') - during this test the existing 'Starting download for ...' line was logged by _startPendingDeps BEFORE the branch decision, so the log looked identical for resume and clean-start and could not confirm the fix; only the byte measurements could.

attention.reason: F1 verified for quit-mid-download. Two gaps left: run the 20-second CANCEL check (start an install, press Cancel, confirm the partial IS deleted) and F4 is diagnosed but unfixed (snapshot handler's clientPending filter at js/services/downloadService.js:391 omits 'installing', so a snapshot wipes a job mid-install; only bites when the model-level complete lags the dep-level one, i.e. big slow installs).
